failure_reason: no benefit detected and monomorphic optimum is not above resident — trait appears to have no evolutionary effect

gates.name: gifted_benefit
gates.question: Does the trait provide an installed benefit when the cost is waived?
gates.interpretation: A gifted/cost-off benefit (delta=-0.1565 < -eps=-1e-06: high trait underperforms low) shows the feature CAN be useful; it does NOT imply the trait is evolvable.  Evolvability requires a positive LOCAL selection gradient near the resident value, not just a global benefit at high trait values.

gates.name: monomorphic_sweep
gates.question: Where does the monomorphic N* landscape peak relative to the resident?
gates.metric: mean N*(h) over seeds for each h in grid
gates.interpretation: Monomorphic global optimum at h*=0.1 with mean N*=546. A global/monomorphic optimum above the resident does NOT prove LOCAL evolvability: the resident might be separated from h* by a fitness valley that rare mutants cannot cross.  Local invasion assays (gates C and D) are required to establish a positive selection gradient near the resident.

gates.name: local_pairwise_gradient
gates.question: Does the mutant h=0.15 invade the resident h=0.1 in head-to-head competition?
gates.metric: invader fraction at end relative to 0.5 neutral start
gates.interpretation: Local pairwise gradient verdict: FLAT_OR_NOISY.  wins=5/8 in ambiguous range [4, 6] or mean_effect not positive (mean_effect=0.09906181449686947).  This tests whether a single-step mutant (h=0.15) can invade a resident (h=0.1) population — the direct test of local evolvability.

gates.name: invasion_from_rarity
gates.question: Can a rare mutant (h=0.15, ~5%) increase in a resident background?
gates.metric: fraction of seeds where mutant frequency increased over the window
gates.interpretation: Invasion from rarity verdict: DOES_NOT_INVADE.  increase_count=0/8 <= lose_threshold=3.  Mutant started at ~5% frequency (invader_count=5).  This is a more stringent test than equal-count pairwise: the mutant must increase when genuinely rare, as required by adaptive dynamics.

gates.name: null_guards
gates.question: Do all null/anti-cheat guards pass?

guards.name: cost_off_disconnected_byte_identical
guards.reason: events_hash identical for h=0.0 and h=0.6 when trait disconnected

guards.name: no_direct_h_reward
guards.reason: assert_no_direct_h_reward passed: enable_thermosense=True, freeze=True, founder_mix set

guards.name: perfect_percept_null
guards.reason: perfect_percept_null requires backend=='thermosense' AND enable_niche=True (a confusion knob).  Got backend='thermosense', enable_niche=False.  Do NOT fake a pass here — this guard must be left NOT_IMPLEMENTED.

guards.name: frozen_memory_map
guards.reason: frozen_memory_map is a documented interface (the creature's learned resource map could substitute for sensor skill), but no freeze_learning_rate check or separate no-memory control run is implemented in this gate.  Left NOT_IMPLEMENTED; enable_band_staleness + freeze_learning_rate is the relevant engine knob.

guards.name: shuffle_order
guards.reason: base_cfg.shuffle_creature_order=True: id-order eat-first confound is neutralised

gates.interpretation: Guard battery: PASS=['cost_off_disconnected_byte_identical', 'no_direct_h_reward', 'trait_disabled_null', 'population_validity', 'shuffle_order']; NOT_IMPLEMENTED/NA=['perfect_percept_null', 'frozen_memory_map'].  all_pass=True (counts only PASS/FAIL; NOT_IMPLEMENTED/NA do not fail).  A positive gradient verdict should be treated as an artifact if any guard FAILs.
